path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\DLHHL.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (DLHHL): volatility-adaptive trend-following system using day-high/low pivot references and rolling N-day average volatility ratios to set dynamic entry thresholds, with graduated position scaling and timed exit near session close.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\HLM.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (HLM): breakout system that tracks the rolling N-day highest-high and lowest-low midpoint, entering long when today's range shifts higher than yesterday's and short when it shifts lower, with incremental add-on entries and a staged timed exit.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\MA.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (MA): dual-moving-average crossover system (5-period vs 60-period) that enters long during day session or overnight when fast MA > slow MA and price exceeds session high average, and shorts only when price is below 5-day closing average, with settlement-day forced close.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\ma+uvdv.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (ma+uvdv): extends the MA crossover system by adding a UVDV composite signal (spread between data2 and data3, likely up-volume vs down-volume) as a filter, dynamically adjusting MA periods based on win-count history for long and short sides separately.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\maimai_fish.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (maimai_fish): mean-reversion DCA system that places an initial limit buy below the 60-bar moving average by N points, then adds two more limit buy layers each 300 points lower, exiting on a 300-point profit from any layer or on settlement day.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\MXNDU.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (MXNDU): volatility-normalized breakout using rolling N-day average of max/min daily distances from computed pivot levels to calibrate entry thresholds, with bar-count-gated stop-loss and trailing take-profit exits after minimum holding period.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\RushBear (2).txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (RushBear): short-side momentum system based on session open vs historical high-open volatility ratio, entering short when price breaks below a pivot computed from session open minus trend-volatility; uses MFE-based profit target and time-gated stop-loss tied to next-session open.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF core): gap-fade mean-reversion system entering long when open < prior close minus N-day avg range and price breaks day high, short vice-versa; exits via 0.5×volRange stop and limit orders with forced market close at session end.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_1.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_1): adaptive trend-following system computing intra-bar slope from 20-day ATR divided by day length, tracking intraday up/down adjustment scores to dynamically shift long/short price thresholds, with trend carry-over across sessions and momentum-based stop triggers.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_20220306.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_20220306): full production SBF with 20-day rolling distance arrays tracking pivot min/max distances, separate long/short start prices, intra-bar up/down adjustment counters, dual lot entries with stop-based add-on, and a staged 5-bar exit ramp.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_ADA.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_ADA): asymmetric slope-filter system using separate bearish (C1/C4) and bullish (C2/C3) bar-count accumulators to gate long/short entries relative to an intraday slope threshold derived from 20-day ATR, with settlement-day and overnight session guards.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_dev.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_dev): experimental version computing average trend/correction velocities per bar over 20 days for bull/bear days separately, uses parabolic (k-squared) position thresholds for a dynamic box boundary, with half-position partial exit when price reverses through the box.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_Ori.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_Ori, original): simplified 1-minute SBF kernel using min/max of open vs prior close as start boundaries and ATR-scaled slope times bar-count for long/short thresholds; clean session-end exit without arrays or adjustment counters.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_ori2.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_ori2): extends SBF_Ori by adding dual intraday dn/upAdj counters that shift threshold prices each bar based on candle structure; uses same 5-bar staged exit ramp with commented-out prior stop logic preserved.

path: E:\@交易\@程式交易\達哥\SBF\SBF_RushBear_chg.txt
summary: MultiCharts PowerLanguage strategy (SBF_RushBear_chg): short-biased variant merging RushBear short-entry logic with SBF session-open/high tracking for long entries; uses MFE-adjusted stop for shorts, 5-bar staged exit, and has long lots disabled by default (lotL1=lotL2=0).

path: E:\@交易\MAE.txt
summary: PowerLanguage utility snippet tracking rolling 100-trade arrays of MAE and MFE normalized by ATR entry range for both long and short sides, computing 3-trade running averages as a strategy performance diagnostics helper.

path: E:\@交易\投資交易心得筆記.txt
summary: Edward's personal trading philosophy notes: market-state segmentation (trend vs range using oscillator/momentum filters), critique of long-only retail courses, analysis of true leverage (mortgage stacked on futures), and inflation-as-tailwind logic for borrowing — synthesizing quantitative and macro views.
